'''''Star Wars'' 14''' is the fourteenth issue of the [[Star Wars Legends|Legends]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series [[Star Wars (1977)|''Star Wars'']]. It was written by [[Archie Goodwin]], illustrated by [[Carmine Infantino]], and first published on [[May 9]], [[1978]] by [[Marvel Comics]]. The issue features the story "'''The Sound of Armageddon!'''," in which the [[Battle of Drexel|battle]] on [[Drexel/Legends|Drexel]] comes to an end.

==Publisher's summary==
''Space pirates are using a sonic jammer to take down any and all ships, whether they're Rebel or Imperial!''

==Plot summary==
On the water world of [[Drexel/Legends|Drexel]], [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Quarg]] has used his sonic jammer to cause the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] to crash into the water near his giant [[Great Ship|city-ship]]. [[Han Solo/Legends|Han]] is believed dead, while [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], and the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] are imprisoned inside of Quarg's galleon. Quarg has simultaneously been waging a war against the [[Dragon Lords]] who even now attack his ship, riding atop giant [[sea-dragon]]s.

[[File:Battle_of_Drexel.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The battle on Drexel]]
Quarg has now turned his sonic jammer towards an even greater prize—a stolen [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Crimson Jack's battlecruiser|battlecruiser]] locked in Drexel's orbit. The pirate, [[Crimson Jack/Legends|Crimson Jack]] and his first mate, [[Jolli]], attempt to break free of their ship's systems-lock, but find that they are trapped.

Han, meanwhile, is not dead at all. The Dragon Lords have rescued him and have taken him to a secret underground cave. The [[Unidentified Dragon Lord|leader]] of the Dragon Lords tells Han that Quarg's sonic jammer is driving the sea serpents mad and slowly killing them. Han agrees to help if they agree to help him in return by salvaging the ''Millennium Falcon''.

Governor Quarg has found [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]] hiding within the ''Millennium Falcon'' and chases her back onto the city-ship. Believing that Han is dead, Chewbacca goes berserk and breaks free of the prison. Luke has been forced into doing Quarg's dirty work until he reunites with Han who explains to him what the sonic jammer is doing to the dragons. Luke rescues Leia and knocks Quarg from the city-ship into the ocean, killing him, just as the jamming equipment is destroyed by the ''Falcon''.

==Development==
[[File:Mswc14coverOmni.jpg|thumb|right|135px|''Star Wars 14'', in ''Star Wars Omnibus'']]
''Star Wars 14'' was written and edited by [[Archie Goodwin]], penciled by [[Carmine Infantino]], inked by [[Terry Austin]], and colored by [[Janice Cohen]], with lettering by [[Denise Wohl]]. Infantino and Austin also illustrated the cover art. The issue was first published by [[Marvel Comics]] on [[May 9]], [[1978]],<ref name="Marvel" /> and a digital edition was released on [[September 29]], [[2015]].<ref name="Comicstore">{{Marvel|subdomain=comicstore|url=Star-Wars-1977-1986-14/digital-comic/37201|text=Star Wars (1977-1986) #14}} {{C|Digital edition}}</ref> The issue has also been collected in several trade paperbacks from both Marvel and [[Dark Horse Comics]]. In the Dark Horse trade paperback [[Star Wars Omnibus: A Long Time Ago.... Volume 1|''Star Wars Omnibus: A Long Time Ago....'' Volume 1]], the cover of ''Star Wars 14'' is pictured with a solid white background, rather than the red vector field.<ref name="Star Wars Omnibus">''[[Star Wars Omnibus: A Long Time Ago.... Volume 1]]''</ref>
